title: Two Records, One Algarve
title_candidates: The Baker and the Bombing Run
theme: A TEG that rewrote the record book at both ends: Alex Baker takes the Trophy with the highest Stableford total in TEG history, while David Mullin's gross golf — opened with a TEG-record +4 at Boavista — delivers a Green Jacket by twelve. Two coronations on different metrics, one week on the Algarve.
tone: house — faithful, tongue-in-cheek, Ronay/Peck-flavoured; warm to the players but quick to needle them
opening_hook: Open on the 18th at Boavista on the final afternoon as Alex Baker signs for 51 points — the best Stableford round in TEG history — then rewind to Mullin's +4 opener and the four-day double coronation that followed.
foreshadow: Mullin's gross +4 at Boavista in R1 — a number that sets the Green Jacket race up as a procession before it's even begun
Alex Baker shadowing the leader from day one (41, then 45) — the chasing pack of one
R3 at Palmares as the swing day: the moment Mullin's eight-shot Trophy cushion finally vanishes
John Patterson, sixth after the first hole he didn't lead, and sixth at every checkpoint thereafter

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Mullin led the Trophy after R1 by eight and after R2 by five, with Alex Baker the patient runner-up on 41 and 45. R3 at Palmares Alvor/Lagos was the pivot: Alex posted a personal-best 47, briefly ceded the outright lead to Gregg Williams at the 13th after a quintuple-bogey 10, then retook it by the 15th and slept on it. The coronation came at Boavista on Sunday, where his record 51-pt round stretched the margin to eleven and lifted his total to a TEG-record 184. Gregg Williams second, Mullin third on the Stableford ladder.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: This was over by Saturday lunchtime. Mullin opened with a gross +4 at Boavista — the best gross round in TEG history — and was never headed, leading by 14, 13, 10 and finally 12. Only Jon Baker (a TEG-3rd-best gross +10 in R2) ever looked like making a fight of it. Mullin's final +51 is also a TEG record for gross total; Jon Baker took the runner-up spot at +63, a personal best.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Wire-to-wire. Patterson was last after four holes of R1 and stayed last for the remaining 68. The route there was paved with disasters: a 10 at Boavista's 8th in R1, a 10 at Palmares's 1st in R2, another 10 at the 8th in R3, and the coup de grâce — an 11 at Boavista's 8th in R4 — finishing with his worst-ever round of 21 points. Total: 112 points, comfortably (uncomfortably) clear at the bottom.

headline_candidates: Four Over and Five Clear: a Statement Opener
The Day Boavista Became a Putting Green (for One Man)
rounds.angle: Mullin's TEG-record gross +4 / 49-pt round opens both competitions in the same stroke; Alex shadows on 41, Patterson is last by the 4th, Neumann finds a 10 at the 18th.

headline_candidates: A New Course, Three PBs and a Sextuple
rounds.angle: TEG's first look at Palmares yields personal bests for Alex (45/+23), Gregg (48/+14) and Jon Baker (gross +10, TEG's 3rd-best ever), while Patterson opens with a 10 and Neumann bleeds 13 shots in three holes.

headline_candidates: Alex Baker, by way of a 10
Three Trophy Leaders in Nine Holes
rounds.angle: The swing round: Alex's PB 47 takes the Trophy lead at the 6th, surrenders it to Gregg at the 13th after a quintuple-bogey 10, then reclaims it by the 15th — and Mullin's eight-shot opening cushion is gone for good.

headline_candidates: 51 — A Number for the Record Book
Alex Baker Crowns It; Patterson Buries It
rounds.chosen_headline: 51 — A Number for the Record Book
rounds.angle: Alex closes with the best Stableford round in TEG history (51 pts / gross +17 PB), with 21 points in a six-hole burst from the 5th. Mullin signs for the Jacket; Patterson signs for an 11 and his worst-ever 21.

players.arc: Patient runner-up turned record-breaker: 41, 45, PB 47, then a TEG-record 51 — four rounds, three personal bests, one Trophy at 184, and a gross PB of +90 to boot.

Opens with the best gross round in TEG history (+4) and a 49-pt Stableford to match; leads the Jacket wire-to-wire and wins it by twelve at a record +51, even as the Trophy slips from his grasp at Palmares.
Posts a PB 48 / gross +14 at Palmares, briefly holds the outright Trophy lead in R3, and ends a clear second on Stableford with a gross PB of +76.
The quiet gross specialist — a TEG-3rd-best +10 at Palmares anchors a personal-best Jacket campaign of +63, runner-up to Mullin.
Last after four holes of R1 and last at every checkpoint thereafter — a 10, a 10, a 10, an 11, and a closing worst-ever 21 deliver the Spoon on 112.
Found Boavista's 18th twice for 10s and contributed a 13-shot collapse over three holes at Palmares; collateral damage in the records race.

venue_notes: TEG's 4th visit to the Algarve (2017), bookended by Boavista (R1 = 11th TEG round here; R4 = 12th — a known quantity, which makes Mullin's +4 and Alex's 51 all the more striking) and two brand-new Palmares combinations in the middle (Praia/Alvor and Alvor/Lagos, both Robert Trent Jones Jr.). Lean on the Atlantic views at Boavista as the backdrop to the bookend records; play Palmares's debut as the moment the tournament cracked open.
